claim_boundary.next_gate: Rank the retained candidates with the transpiler in the optimization loop before preregistering a new disjoint-seed layout/readout holdout.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: Improved verifier completeness, readout mitigation, current calibration, provider access, hardware execution, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or B10 credit.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Deterministic calibration-aware physical-layout candidates and compiled exposure comparisons for the fixed R125 tasks.

requirements.label: R125 and R126 source artifacts are hash-bound and consumed
all injective six-to-seven physical mappings are enumerated
the static objective uses only snapshot and task-graph properties
ten ranked mappings are retained per snapshot/task
one selected mapping is transpiled per snapshot/task
selected compiled circuits preserve logical classical-bit order
compiled exposure is compared against every R125 default layout
no acceptance holdout or readout mitigation is executed
historical snapshots remain separate from current and hardware evidence
no soundness, advantage, BQP, or new credit is claimed
